title: NMR Serum Sample Preparation for NMR metabolomics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"k9iv","text":"This is a modified protocol to conduct NMR metabolomics for serum samples using methanol extraction. guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9dqq0","text":"Frozen samples should be thawed in    cold room or on ice. All sample preparation has to be done in    cold room or on ice. steps: In a 250 ml volumetric flask, dissolve 2321.55 mg of anhydrous NaH 2 PO 4 and 802.25 mg of Na 2 HPO4 in 200 mL of D2O.
Add 600 µL of NMR buffer to each sample to resuspend the pellets
Thaw the sample on ice or in 4 °C
Dry the samples in a speed-vac concentrator (time depends on the sample condition) Store the dry pellets in -80 °C if needed
Transfer the supernatant of each sample to a new tube
Centrifuge the samples at 16000 rcf for 00:30:00
Vortex the samples and incubate the samples in -20 °C for 00:20:00
Add MeOH to samples on ice at a 3:1 methanol-to-sample ratio 750 µL MeOH 250 µL neat sera for pre-challenge samples 1200 µL MeOH 400 µL near sera for post-challenge samples
Thaw samples on ice or at 4 °C
Add 833.25 µL of 0.1 DSS-D6 stock solution (1/3 of 1mM DSS when mixed with sample)
Adjust the pH to pH 7.0 using HCL or NaOH
Adjust the volume to 250 mL with D 2 O and mix well
creator: Deanna Lanier
